parsed_review.summary: CP00-429 is a clean descriptor-only pack covering the RP14.P02.M03 Primary Implementation Slice tail (8 rows, S15-S22) and the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice head (2 rows, S01-S02) over exactly 10 planned RP14 units. The pack-level production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false claim is honest and fully supported: manifest, command-evidence, registry, service, validators, tests, and contract are mutually consistent. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold — the attestation chain reports all runtime-dispatch/state-write/audit-write/object-storage/Hermes-runtime-receipt/command-runtime flags false, every emitted row is descriptor_only with runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, and raw_payload_included=false, and validateSettlementCoreCp429P02ImplementationWorkflowSliceDescriptor enforces each of these plus the authority boundary. Authority boundary is correct: authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, and attestation promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false / claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false — Claude remains a read-only reviewer (allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob) and not final approval authority, with enterprise-trust closure reserved to a human outside this receipt. Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic descriptor/evidence-packet strings (H14 gate, receipt_shape, production_ready_candidate = coverage.valid && slice.valid); no product runtime is embedded. CP178+ review-evidence hardening is satisfied: auth_preflight passed (exit_code 0, 1436-byte non-empty valid JSON), prompt 2191<25600 bytes with no full diff/source, forbidden_tools_present=false, and the inherited forbidden_review_evidence list rejects auth failures, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and missing fields. Coverage is drift-checked end-to-end (risk A, deliverable_counts ui1/impl2/failure_recovery2/test4/contract1, micro-phase counts M03:8/M04:2) with M03 S15-S22 continuing CP00-428's S01-S14 with no gaps or overlaps. Two non-blocking P3 maintainability observations carry over from prior packs; no P0/P1/P2 issues found.

findings.title: Inert ROW_EXTRAS inheritance in CP429 descriptor builder (carryover of CP00-428-P3-01)
findings.details: SETTLEMENT_CORE_CP429_ROW_EXTRAS spreads SETTLEMENT_CORE_CP428_ROW_EXTRAS, which transitively (CP428<-CP427<-CP426) only ever defines the keys scope_inventory, acceptance_gate_definition, non_goal_boundary, and documentation_entry — all CP426-era row slugs. None of these match any CP429 row key (approval_required_routing, blocked_claim_output, rollback_behavior, retry_behavior, unit_test_happy_path/denied_path/review_path, integration_smoke_case, service_entrypoint_contract, request_normalization), so the per-row spread '...(SETTLEMENT_CORE_CP429_ROW_EXTRAS[key] ?? {})' at service.js:497 always resolves to {}. The inherited map is dead weight; emitted descriptor rows carry only the base fields (title, descriptor_only, runtime_execution, customer_safe_errors_only, raw_payload_included, section_micro_phase_id). Purely a readability/maintainability nit with zero functional effect on the frozen descriptor output or any boundary guarantee.
findings.recommendation: In a future maintainability pass, either drop the inert ROW_EXTRAS spread for CP429 or guard it with a key-intersection assertion so an empty intersection is made explicit. No change required for closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking; deferred to optional future cleanup. Does not affect the production_ready=false posture or any no-write/descriptor-only guarantee.

findings.title: Cumulative no_write_attestation retains base-pack validates_scope_contract_foundation label
findings.details: SETTLEMENT_CORE_CP429_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION spreads the CP428->CP427->CP426 attestation chain, so the CP429 object accumulates all four cumulative descriptor-scope flags: validates_scope_contract_foundation_descriptor_only (CP426 origin), validates_p01_closeout_p02_foundation_descriptor_only (CP427), validates_p02_contract_implementation_slice_descriptor_only (CP428), and validates_p02_implementation_workflow_slice_descriptor_only (CP429). All flags are true and the object is synced to the contract's cp429_no_write_attestation/no_write_attestation blocks, so there is no false or weakened attestation. The only issue is labeling clarity: the base-pack 'scope_contract_foundation' flag semantically describes CP426's scope, which can mildly mislead a reader inspecting the CP429 attestation in isolation.
findings.recommendation: Optionally narrow each pack's attestation to its own validates_* flag (or add a single validates_scope summary field) so the attestation self-describes per pack. Cosmetic only; safe to defer.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking; deferred. Every boolean is accurate (true) and contract-synced; no impact on no-write/no-real-data guarantees, the authority boundary, or production_ready.
